channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: You MUST post in this thread if you are waging rn — 31 replies — 124.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You MUST post in this thread if you are waging rn

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-11 23:06 UTC

OP

wage

Early replies (first 3)

62351330
I'm currently hiding in the bathroom

62351353
>>62351325
Im not desu

62351358
>>62351325
i hate being a wagie so fucking much

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62351383
>>62351358
>SIRGAY NOOOOOOOOOOOO!

62351407
>>62351330
I have been in the bathroom for 2 hours. Only 19 minutes until my shift ends

62351780
>>62351325
They fired a jeet the other day for playing world of tanks on the shitter

62351843
>>62351325
Sitting on a golf cart watching my stocks crab

62351868
I haven't worked in exactly one year. I'm about to fuck my gf and go to bed now (Thailand time zone).

62351890
55/hr to sit on a construction site for 8 hrs a day. About 1 hrs worth of paperwork a day. I'm slowly losing my mind but trying to stick it out until my portfolio is big enough to NEET off of.

62351902
>>62351325
im getting paid like $50 an hour right now and siting in my underwear

62352109
reporting in from my remote cage

62352139
Hiding in my car I don't feel like working today. Definitely don't see the point in it anymore!

62352173
>>62351325
I don't want to but I have to
> wage

62352194
>>62351330
Work bathroom chads reporting

62352340
>>62351325
>>62351890
Just woke up. Drinking tea and playing Rainbow 6 Siege. Making $100 an hour. Made $800 sleeping 8 hours last night.

62352347
>>62351325
Just fuck my shit up senpai

62352360
>>62351325
Hi bros
>>62352340
How?

62352395
Prosecutor. Wasting time at work. Waiting for tomorrow to buy another $4000 in LINK when my paycheck lands. Feels good man.

62352400
I am employed but I’m not working today.

62352412
>>62352400
So you have a random Thursday off?
So you don’t even work a white collar 9-5? ngmi.

62352476
>>62351325
been waging since 8am EST. going to be here until 7pm. this is day 4 of 4 for the week thank god.

t. family doctor

62352824
Post your wagiestation

62352850
>>62352412
I am off Thursday and Friday. work from home Saturday through Wednesday.

62352871
>>62352824
>ping 192.168.254.85
>three times
what are u doing

62352886
>>62352871
I pinged once and it replied 4 times. That what it do.

62353251
>>62351325
Does it count as waging if I work from home at whatever hours I want, but still paid by the hour?

62353364
>>62352824
You work for DXC or Ash Grove Cement company?

62353387
>>62352824
Yeah seems I can find most of your colleagues LinkedIn etc from your teams picture.

62353397
done waging
+$800
I'm underpaid for this shit

62353400
My employment status to QPS is [EMPLOYMENT]: Unknown.

62353572
>>62351325
Please end my suffering.

item.title: NUCLEAR HAPPENING — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NUCLEAR HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-11 23:04 UTC

OP

COINBASE NOW ALLOWS AI AGENTS TO TRADE CRYPTO AUTONOMOUSLY

Early replies (first 3)

62352704
Institutions were doing this 4+ years ago, will change nothing.

62352713
Who care Robin hood did it first

Also I think thos agentic stuff might be overhyped, I ran bots before and idk why I would switch to an ai that hallucinates

62352715
>>62352687
Sharper /pol/ + /b/‑style mock

“NUCLEAR HAPPENING”
Calm down, doomsday prophet — Coinbase letting AI trade crypto isn’t the Cuban Missile Crisis.

You’re acting like ChatGPT just seized the launch codes when all it did was place a limit order on DOGE at 3 AM.

This isn’t Skynet, it’s a robo‑intern buying your bags because you can’t stop doomscrolling long enough to click “confirm trade.”

The only meltdown here is your brain trying to process a press release.

Even sharper (still safe)

Bro saw “AI agents can rebalance your portfolio” and immediately started building a fallout shelter out of Amazon boxes.

Coinbase: adds a convenience feature

OP: “THE MACHINES HAVE RISEN.”

Relax, Nostradamus. The AI isn’t overthrowing humanity — it’s just making sure you stop panic‑selling at the bottom.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62352728
i will outcompete them easily

market buy @40K
market sell @180K

I'm the most handsome man on 4chan

62352738
>>62352687
AI can't do that yet, so "agent" is the correct word, i.e. a model that can't trade gets stupid bandaid skills context from users, in the hope to bait them into losing money.
First thing I did with the new v5 model from Anthropic is stock assessment, it's still too weakly willed to form an opinion, it still just mirrors your sentiment.

62352773
>>62352687
nobody cares about "muh crypto" any more boomer. wake up

62352788
>>62352773

8
shadow
not first edition

into the trash it goes

62352838
>>62352788
k ill take it out of your trash

62352840
>>62352788
Should I get it graded?

62352936
>>62352773
>nobody cares
Pretty much this. Not even coinbase has that kind of pull anymore to get people’s attention. Base was OK in the beginning but it turned out to be yet another liquidity farm for insiders. For years, crypto had a bad rep but the last couple of years, along with the sol shitcoin casino, basically finished it off.

62352979
>>62352773
>now the cryptards will try to kill pokemon for threatening their ecosystem

62353423
>>62352773
You really need that faggot chain to work out huh. You've been shilling for the last week. KYS people on this board trade metals,crypto, and stock. That is a mentally retarded tranny "asset" I will NEVER view artificial scarcity trading cards as an investment.

62353565
>>62352687
this was happening their first year of the Coinbase foundation in 2015, moron.

item.title: spaceX IPO — 18 replies — 51.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
spaceX IPO

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-11 22:46 UTC

OP

are we ready for the biggest scam in history of histories?

ELON MUSK, YOU FAGGGOT

when will your ponzi scheme finally collapse, you fucking hobo

Early replies (first 3)

62351469
>>62351461
like 8 different brokers and crypto apps have shilled this shit to me fucking lmao

are they gonna rape retail instantly or let it pump for a few days before the raping?

62351500
It's actually going to be a repeat of the ICP ico isn't it?

62351509
>>62351469
>are they gonna rape retail instantly or let it pump for a few days before the raping?
supposedly this is entering indexes in 15 days

total scam of scams. can't believe this is fucking happening. everyone (ameriburgers) about to get raped

i'm not american but I would've voted for the faggot. but this...
it would've either been: continuation of woke lunacy or scams

hard times
>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AEB6ibtdPZc

>are they gonna
the cult is real. it will pump and potentially crater with ai bust in 6-12 months (or god knows when or if)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62351513
SpaceX IPO is literally the end of the stock market in both name and theory for the rest of all human history

62351521
>>62351461
Gaps to 300 on the first day, easily

62351530
>>62351461
21 hours till elon becomes worlds first trillionaire

62351534
>>62351461
I will post my gains here tomorrow.

62351545
>>62351461
>>62351500
>>62351513
Thanks bros, I just sold 10k off my portfolio to buy in :)

62351548
>>62351461
>Elmo Elongated Muskrat company bad
You do know that reddit has a stock board and they'll even give you reddit gold for saying such brave things?

62351550
>>62351509
>(or god knows when or if)
ai bust is going to happen

62351553
>>62351521
Slippage is gonna be insane on second one. I don’t see SPCX pricing below 200 after the first minute. Be sure to have a sizeable lump of cash if you are YOLOing.

62351559
>>62351548
>>Elmo Elongated Muskrat company bad
log off this board please

reddit and all liberal faggots were glazing this guy until he went Trumpster

MAGA BABY

62351572
>>62351530
whats even the point, the stupid nigger just works 24/7 and is CEO of 5 companies for fun or something who knows

like go spend your trillion dollars with those 10 kids and help them with their homework and take them on trips with the Private jet nigga wtf?? buy some 600M dollar mega yacht and have the kids go crazy while crossing the Atlantic on a mega mansion boat. Maybe find a nice woman your age to date and spend time with her too

62351576
>>62351548
about the only good thing this faggot did, was to put all the liberal dollars he received to semi decent use. compared to the lunacy on the other side
other than that, he's a mega shameful-autist scammer

62351581
>>62351572
He wants to build an army + his own country. He wants to liberate us from our oppressive governments. Poorfags like you wouldn’t understand

62351589
>>62351581
i guess bro

62351702
Surely it will go up for a brief moment once trading opens right?

62353489
I was around for the RIVIAN ipo in November 2021.
This is gonna be a similar shitshow

item.title: I Love Silver, BRICs Shills Like Peter Schiff are Wrong — 16 replies — 35.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
I Love Silver, BRICs Shills Like Peter Schiff are Wrong

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-11 22:40 UTC

OP

The worse thing about my journey buying silver are all the shills who talk about the "American Empire" and how China and Russia will rise up and replace it.

Not happening.

I'm Chinese. My partner's family is Chinese. Everyone we know wants to leave China. It's just as shit as the rest of the world right now, but it also lacks a lot of history due to communists destroying everything. It's not taking over anything any time soon.

Also, Iran is getting it's assed kicked. Iran is like some retard that is in a street fight with an MMA fighter. The MMA fighter has the retard pinned to the ground, telling him to relax and calm down, while the retard keeps yelling, saying he would kill the MMA fight if he let him stand up. Iran is the retard in this story.

I buy silver because it is objectively money. Solar panels are fake. AI is overhyped. BRICs is gay and is losing to Ukraine and Israel. Chinese video games suck. Stop giving gay reasons why silver should be valuable. Silver is valuable because it is a commodity that doesn't decay and is fungible. Aliens would recognize gold, silver, and platinum as money, not Bitcoin or fiat cash.

There is no good reason to short silver right now. Central banks are net buyers, and Indian/Arabs/Chinese. I look forward to the silver price in a few months.

INB4 "I'm not reading all of that"

Early replies (first 3)

62349720
>>62349655
Ok. But my 5kg of silver now has me at a loss.

62349723
>>62349720
That's because you're a reactionary, who bought a peek. Buy more as it goes down. Or don't, less demand for the shiny, means more shiny for me. I don't give a fuck.

62349725
>>62349723
I bought last year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349761
>>62349723
I wish I could like comments on 4chan, because then I would like yours.

All the negative sentiment on silver makes me think we're about to have a huge upswing. Trump is now bombing Iran, which will probably force them into a deal.

If they don't make a deal, the US will just keep bombing them. They already don't control the straight. Oil is getting through. This war is the biggest nothingburger of all time.

62349782
why is there so much posting of gold and silver now.

>>62349723
>dood
>cheapies
lol
Retard

62350748
>>62349725
Really? You bought last year when silver ranged from the low 30s to the low 70s, then managed to sit through the last 5 months of supra $75 silver and you 'lost' money?

62351148
>>62349782
>so much posting of gold and silver now
Because eventually the USD interest debt will pass tax revenue.
Crypto is a fiat derivative along with of course stocks and bonds. Eventually people will realize how much of a scam fiat currency is and will go all in on the asset class that has been money for the last 6,000+ years of recorded human history, precious metals.

62351162
>>62351148
Okay so more useless doomer idiot posts from some fag that has been posting the squeeze squoze shit for 10 years, gotcha.

62351864
>>62349720
Imagine measuring your stack in fiat rather than weight. Retard.

62351893
>>62349655
>Aliens would recognize gold, silver, and platinum as money

There are millions of dumb people out there that would gobble up precious metals if you convinced them of that.

62352071
>>62349655
>China is bad
>They buy silver so its valuable
That's self contradictory. You should buy treasury bills.

62352113
>>62351162
I got rich by buying silver!

62352216
>>62349761
Why wouldn't aliums want radioactive materials or osmium/iridium instead?

62353392
>>62352216
They might want those things but they wouldn't use them as currency. Gold and silver are nearly indestructible. They are created inside a dying star or from stars colliding. That is why they are used as currency.

62353439
>>62351864
5kg

5 k g

kilos

retard

r e t a r d

62353464
>>62350748
I bought at the end of last year

item.title: Reminder that XRP bagholders have absolutely nothing to say when questioned on why their token should or would ever incr… — 20 replies — 22.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Reminder that XRP bagholders have absolutely nothing to say when questioned on why their token should or would ever incr…

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-11 22:13 UTC

OP

Reminder that XRP bagholders have absolutely nothing to say when questioned on why their token should or would ever increase in value (they just say kaboom or accuse you of owning an equally shitty crypto)

Early replies (first 3)

62345876
>>62345842
KABOOOOOOOM YOU HOLD CHAINLINK OOOOOOOOOOOKOKKKKKKK

62345879
>>62345876
And lo it was as foretold

62345931
>>62345842
Reminder that <literally any cryptocuckshit> bagholders have absolutely nothing to say when questioned on why their token should or would ever increase in value (they just say kaboom or accuse you of owning an equally shitty crypto)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62345936
>>62345931
Fair. Monero or privacy crypto could have been world changing, except you can never cash out or buy anything with it

62345958
>>62345936
By design.

62345960
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62345970
most of the so called utility coins have been around for 14-15 years, if they were actually going to do something they would have 10 times already. Even the fucking dotcom boom crashed and recovered in half the time. What else is there to wait for? Clarity Act? Don't make me laugh.

62346026
>>62345970
You could have held Walmart and apple for 20 years before it really took off, 12 years for Amazon…so you’re a retard

62346045
>>62345960
Does nothing for the xrp price

62346051
>>62345960
>30+ partners

62347125
>>62346045
I’ve been holding XRP for years, but no single good news has pumped its prices. Even when XRP got clarity in court, its price only went up measly 40 cents before it dumped back down. You can declare XRP intergalactic bridge currency, but it will still dump or crab. This is because they’re using bots to suppress the price, which has been documented for a long time.

62349844
>>62347125
>hurr xrp doesn't pump on news and is suppressed
You're an idiot.
XRP did a 2x after the lawsuit ended, and Bitcoin even pumped to help it.

62350438
>>62349844
>You're an idiot
>BTC/USDT chart

62350641
Back then, at $3.20 in 2018
>xrp is going to go to a gorillion dollars because everything will have to use it!
Now, at $1.15
>um ackshually competition is healthy we never claimed we'd run world finance, it's no big deal that everywhere xrp shows up its in a list alongside a bunch of things we claim are ponzis, because we have no moat or exclusivity that matches our original retarded claims... surely it will go to a gorillion dollars anyway!

Lmfao

62350676
>>62345960
Listed alongside ave, solana, and lovable!
Now THATS what I call an xrp monopoly
BOOOOOOOOOOM

62350963
>>62350438
>and Bitcoin even pumped to help it

readin be hard n shiet yo

62351911
>>62350963
I said the price only went up measly 40 cents before it dumped back down. However, the price actually went less from 45c to 76c, then back to 40 cents the next day. Even if this was 2x, it would still be under a dollar after being declared non-security. Random shit crypto after win like this would shot up past two dollars minimum. Fucking brainded retarded monkey nigger.

62352005
>>62345842
It was always a scam but at least it keeps the worst of us poor
>imagine buying the crypto bankers supposedly would use

62352186
>>62345842
>>62345876
>brings up chainlink every time xrp is criticized
why do they do this

62353366
>>62352186
Because xrpaypigs cope by comparing themselves to cucklink and couldn't possibly imagine that *everybody* laughs at them.

item.title: MFW fudders tell me not to buy SPCX — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
MFW fudders tell me not to buy SPCX

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-06-11 23:05 UTC

OP

MFW fudders tell me not to buy SPCX

Early replies (first 3)

62352888
>>62352829
Is this the thread?

62353466
>>62352829
Go all in then lol, see what happens.
>>62352888
Yep. Nothing of value would be lost if jannies nuke this thread

62353568
>>62352888
Nakadashi Kuuko.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Is using leverage for bitcoin possible/worth it for a long term cyclical investor? — 4 replies — 6.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is using leverage for bitcoin possible/worth it for a long term cyclical investor?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-11 22:29 UTC

OP

Is using leverage for bitcoin possible/worth it for a long term cyclical investor?

I’ve always thought the fees on perps would eat you up but there’s gotta be another way right? I’m european so CFDs? I’m not sure I trust Saylor enough to justify MSTR. I only want like 2-3x lev

Early replies (first 3)

62350042
>>62349504
If you are poor like me you can probably use a 0% credit card. Put all your spending, bills etc on the card and put your cashflow onto bitcoin.

Was able to get a £15k card 4-5 years back. I stuck it all into stocks instead of crypto at the time, but was still able to make a killing

62351642
>>62349504
>CFDs
dear lord just kill yourself now
[spoiler]don't [/spoiler]

62353390
>>62351642
I know they’re somehow sketchy I was just thinking what options there are

>>62350042
Idk seems like it could end in really bad debt really quick

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62353420
>>62349504
Honest answer, ignore those retards. Long? I don't know, it's complicated, unless you have a good amount of margin/collateral that could sustain a black swan (which seems pretty likely in our economy right now), then yes, it's worth it. Fees on perps actually don't eat up as much as you think, it floats, sometimes you pay, sometimes people pay you, at least that's the model on HL.

Now regarding 2-3x, again, unless you can buy the very bottom and still hold up bitcoin under 20k, then it's worth it, but there's still a risk at play.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 191 replies — 16.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 191 • Updated: 2026-06-11 11:46 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62338077
>>62337475
linkpool made us think we could back in the day, it was all a scam, we all had a running node setup and we were waiting to be whitelisted, what a fking joke

62340215
>>62336936
You're misrepresenting my point to argue something else. Why are you doing this? You get nothing out of it unless you're a paid shill or just bored.

62340223
>>62337475
We tried. It's a full time job requiring a bunch of resources on top of the profits being nebulous thus souring a lot of people out of running their node [on AWS]. This was back in 2019 so I don't know how robust and improved running nodes is nowadays.

62340231
>>62340215
I argued against your point that "Chainlink paying the nodes from their own team wallets" decouples the link price from the usage; and then you literally asked me to refute your point that Chainlink doesn't pay the nodes from the uncirculating supply, and is instead sending the Link directly to Binance.

What the fuck are you even doing here?

62340287
>>62340231
> BTC
> No new BTC enters the market except through miners' hands.
> This means they decide and influence the price of BTC.
> This couples the BTC price to the usage of the network because miners will not sell less than the costs of operations

> Link
> new Link enters the market through payments to node operators, payments to developers, and to deals with companies
> Link node operators are no longer the sole arbiters who decide when new Link enters the market
> To access services from the network, you don't need to pay in Link, and your payments made aren't immediately converted to Link, if at all. This decouples the link token from accurately reflecting network activity
> Furthermore CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements, Ex: China's response to the Trump tariffs

https://etherscan.io/tx/0x6e347aa07b74b2330b8f3f5a9da44305cba0bf7e40b416bcf00ba9922c365fdd
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x3355d51eafaead23e063aba8a099a712a11ecb26e937f53d0850cd4c5b33801e
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x2e3b0111c94d9d0102e5822ccd9dc5f395c5b207b39a466432c1d634c4354eb6

^Above transactions took place just an hour before the great crash of October


Your job is to refute my conclusion that the link token price is NOT tied to network usage. Refute these points. You are free to disregard the BTC comparison since those are points in my favor unless you can refute my points. Any thing other than directly addressing and showcasing flaws in my logic (and I better hear something better than the low hanging fruit of "Node operators have to sell Link for cost operations too!") and I will respond with "token not needed".

62340304
>>62340287
You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.
This is what I refuted.
I pointed this out to you many times but you keep pretending not to understand or something.

62340325
>>62340304
>You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.

token not sneeded

62340347
>>62340325
>>62340287
>CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements
This is horseshit by the way.
He has always sold on Fridays, pretty much around the same hour, and always within 3 to 4 month intervals.

Sometimes Link pumps right after (many times in fact), sometimes some shit happens right after. But his timing is fully transparent.

62340403
>>62340347
Anon, the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do in response to Trump's tariffs. For the uninitiated, Trump proposed a 100% tariff on China and China responded with crashing the market. You can dig into the archives to verify. Type in either "Trump", "China" "Binance", "Link" or whatever and date it to October.

62340410
Fuck you chainlinkies!

Sincerely,

The whole XRP community

62340414
>>62340403
>the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do
Those links are the transactions Sergey made on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.
Which has been his unlocking regimen since forever.

62340536
>>62340287
If (almost) no one uses the network, will the token price change or remain (almost) unchanged?

62340953
>>62340347
>But his timing is fully transparent.


classic boyscout bold faced lie

there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales other than them doing it 4 times per year. often it will be completely different months as years past.

didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol

62340971
>>62340953
>there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales
- always around 7% of total supply annually
- always on Fridays
- always within 3 to 4 month intervals

62342184
>>62340953
>didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol
Those are the transactions I posted that >>62340971 is trying to brush off as "part of the plan"

He's moving the goal post even when this discussion just bolsters my point about the link token price not accurately reflecting network usage. I initially wasn't thinking of the "routine token unlocks" so I'll give you my thanks.

62342243
>>62342184
You're claiming he unlocked right before the 10/10 crash to "capitalize on market movements".

The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval. Like he's done for years.

62342296
>>62342243
Oh no no no, he most certainly did unlock just before the crash. It was part of the panic and it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis.

62342402
>>62342296
>he most certainly did unlock just before the crash
Who said he didn't?

>it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis
It was luck BECAUSE he'd already been selling on a 3-4 month basis for years at that point.

62342424
Reminder you must only say positive things, otherwise you are a shill.

62342440
>>62342424
say something nice about chainlink

62342501
>>62313866
>>62313871
Glad to see some of us are still here... I didn't do the presale but t0 Binance buyer.
It's absolutely grim the state of this project.

62342517
>>62342243
>The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.

yeah they do it once a quarter
this particular one we're talking about just happened to be directly before one of the largest selloffs in industry history

>but duuuuuuuuude he always does it like that

no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months. so no it is not just like previous years.

62342559
>>62342517
>no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months
That's because he's unlocking every 3 to 4 months. Not in the same month every time.
He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.

62342602
>dumps straight on the most shady CEX are "unlocks"
peak doublespeak

62344923
>>62342602
All of Chainlink's tokens go through Binance, including node payments.
Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

62346060
>>62342559
>He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.


not sure why you keep repeating this as if this implies some transparency.

>duuuuuuuuuude just hang out on the blockchain every friday and you'll see it coming

its ok to concede they don't want to be transparent here. if they did they would just post a set schedule.

>>62344923
>Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

no one falls for this outside of the biggest retard boyscouts
where do the coins go?

62346092
>>62346060
Unlocking the same amount annually for years, within the same interval for years, and even around the same time of day for years implies transparency, yes.

>where do the coins go?
among others to the nodes

62346134
link blowz

62349168
Posting in the best LINK thread now. Some good convos. I hope we all make it.

62350386
>>62349168
its actually starting to reverbate with the muh influencers, they actuall read this thread, it's a good thing my goal is reached im happy

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 65 replies — 14.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-06-11 18:28 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62345772
>>62345299
Is 4year cycle even real?

62345775
>>62345127
40 years from now

62345776
>>62339328
I sold a small amount at 110k last year, bought a car. Unironically I felt bad once it started pumping back to 120k. Now my car has holded it's value better than BTC.

62345783
>>62345776
Car price depreciated. Nothing special

62345787
>>62341032
I just want x1000 . Utility is nothing

62345800
>>62345600
centralised services doesn't really give me a good impression

62345806
When to sell?

62345827
>>62339328
>Btc
>Eth
>Hbar
>Neo
>Etc
>Kava
>Rose
>Inj

62346229
>>62344890
Yep. Make sure you ignore the fiat value. If it falls to zero, BTC is a new way of finance independent from the outdated monetary system. Be grateful you are part of the revolution. I too will soon enter BTC paradise. Any day now...

62346754
>>62345800
Anon, where did I say anything about centralized services? You're arguing against something I never said. Read up on their TBVs and maybe it'll click.

62346765
>>62339730
That's understandable. It's hard to judge past decisions based on outcomes we couldn't have known at the time.

62349025
>>62346229
Btc will never go to zero. Stop smoking

62349066
>>62345827
oldie bags but gold . Hodl

62349435
>>62346754
Onchain is the game

62349439
>>62345806
Depends on you lmao

62349795
>>62339328
If I had sold to pay off debt, buy a house, or improve my life, I wouldn't regret it. You can't eat unrealized gains.

62350085
>>62345787
go for shitcoins like memecoins on solana

62350154
>>62339328

Buy Polkadot

https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2025/03/establishment-of-the-strategic-bitcoin-reserve-and-united-states-digital-asset-stockpile/

62350283
>>62349435
Yeah. Going on-chain is one thing. Now let's see if the infrastructure can back it up. The public testnet on Aave V4 will speak for itself.

62350284
>>62350154
What happened to polkadot?

62350290
>>62345050

62350315
>>62350290
Ethereum is king

62351078
>>62350154
it's hurried from hell

62351090
>>62350283
Aave performs well as a DeFi platform, but the token itself is struggling.

62351106
>>62339883
Taking profits is never a mistake. Calling the end of crypto has historically been a much tougher bet.

62351114
>ETHEREUM

62351129
>>62345772
It's real until macro, ETFs, institutions, and changing market structure decide otherwise.

62351159
>>62339328
>Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move
It's the definition of a bottom.

62352277
>>62351090
Hmmm, some people are here for the product, some are here for the chart. They're not always the same crowd.

62352286
>>62350315
Ethereum has certainly built one of the strongest ecosystems in crypto.

OP

BOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

THE CLARITY ACT PASSED COMMITTEE.

THE FLOOR IS NEXT.

Early replies (first 3)

62347535

62347592
i dont give a fuck at all.

62347605

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
>the xrp guys here think the boom meme is made up and use it vs link in a cringe way

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62347613

62347652
>>62347515

62347656
MASTERCARD X RIPPLE

BOOOOOOOM! * * * * *

62350310

item.title: Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. — 60 replies — 9.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off.

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-06-11 16:47 UTC

OP

Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. I told him that was good.

I told him that most people will not make it in today’s economy when $150,000 is considered middle class. He replied that $150,000 is a lot of money. I said sure, but the average American salary is around $65,000.

Then I asked him what kind of work he did, and he said he did some low level blue collar welding. I told him blue collar work is terrible. He responded that welders can make over $100,000 a year.

I told him that blue collar work is for low-IQ, uneducated people and that the adults in those jobs are losers and people who messed up their lives. I also said blue collar work is fun when you’re a kid, but the adults doing it are there because they’re losers.

It turned out that three blue collar workers had been listening to our conversation and told the kid not to listen to me because I didn’t know what I was talking about.

I then told the kid that when he grows up, he will realize people look down on blue collar workers for a reason and that blue collar adults are not the kind of people he should want to know.

The blue collar workers called me ignorant, yet, from my perspective, they seemed to have chips on their shoulders and came across as unintelligent.

Why are blue- collar workers like this? Are they aware of how the world perceives them?

I wanted to talk numbers because no way most blue collar makes over $100k. Can someone fill me in?

Early replies (first 3)

62342722
>>62342700
Fuck off

62342735
>>62342700
I like this spam, because it counters the AI brainwashing (trades good, factory good, college bad, IT arrogant & overpaid or Indians, close the borders).

62342744
>>62342735
What?
>>62342722
Fuck you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62343166
>>62343110
Not really. Better than him not doing shit all summer. He already has to take 5 days off.

62343167
>>62342798
>da booooooots
Kek

62343175
>>62343166
child labor is worse than not doing shit all summer. At that age he should study, fuck and do drugs instead of adopting the goy mentality

62343255
>>62343175
There's absolutely zero things wrong with child labor when it involves doing normal ass jobs adults do with the same standards.

People always jump to kids being kept as slaves or beaten or made to carry explosives or work in some dangerous factory without training and it's stupid.

There's zero reason why a 12 year old kid shouldn't be alloweded to get paid to wipe down tables at a McDonald's or bus tables at Applebee's over his summer.

62343264
>>62342700
Had a "white collar" semi-richfag come to me one time for help. He was scared to death to open the radiator cap so he could add water and I told him how to check by pressing the upper hose to see whether there was any pressure or not. Come to find out he didn't even know where to add more water, he didn't know what the radiator cap was or how to open it but was adamant about how dangerous it was so he wouldn't do it. How do these people drive a car?

62343298
>>62342700
What happens when you sit on ifunny and read too many monarchtrump posts, gay ass op get off the board

62343302
>>62343264
they live comfortable lives and any discomforts can be solved with money

62343318
>>62343255
Your lack of education is showing.

62343323
Daily autistic browncel thread

62343328
>>62342700
Stop making these awful fucking threads

62343411
>>62343318
You are a fucking redditor.
>>62343328
Shut up.

62344022
>>62342700
I work a blue collar job.
No overtime
Air conditioned factory
I get a nice comfy chair
Basically watch a CNC machine all day.
Will be bumped up to $139,000 in 2027.
Feels good.

62344029
I guess im technically a blue collar? I work in a factory kinda supervising trades but I dont really do anything stuff myself. 250k tho.

62344036
The elites in power want the actual workers keeping civilization alive poor because they know what is to come.
>sit in a office all day? 300k!!
>create and fix everything? 40k and you should make less!
Always destroy anything you can when fixing these fuckers houses.

62344177
>>62344022
But that’s not even middle class.

62344183
>>62344029
Yeah, still blue collar unfortunately.

62344186
>>62344036
Not really, a fourth worlder can come into usa and do the job.

62344274
>>62342735
I think it's funny

62344383
>>62344274
My threads are funny? Wtf do you mean? What is so funny about my threads?

62344425
fuck trade school. anyone smart should learn to vibe code an app that uses crypto.

62344611
>>62342770
Those people are ether:
1. Retired
2. Trust fund leeches
Now you might say, “nuh uh, they are not parasitic blood sucking invertebrates”
And to that I would say, yes they are.
Money has to be spread out even to the non contributing members and often times instead of focusing on growing their portion; they spend energy on stealing it from the producing members.
About 1/4 of every family is productive.
About 1/2 are ambitious (not profitable)
And 4/4 are living luxuriously
That formula produces 1 producer, 2 layabouts and 1 thief; on average, in every generation.
Basically somebody has to be making the money wether it’s a company ran by the one ambitious and smart family member or my tax dollars in which case you are only advocating for leeching off of somebody and floating the idea it should be me and most people on this board.
So please fuck off and kys, immediately
Please

62344720
You know biz is in dire straits when it's gone the way of /ck/ with so many bot threads to generate a false sense of activity.... Remember to sage these threads

62344811
>>62342770
How poorfag coded are you people that you think just maxing out a couple of credit cards is a lot of money? Like 10-20k might sound like " a lot of money", but even if you aren't rich thats like a year or two of savings, how is that shit going to last you your entire life?

62344833
You won't make it as a blue collar wagie but that doesn't mean its a dead end

Open your own business and build, in the end youre the boss of blue collar wagies and make more than 100k, blue collar work is high in demand and always will, someone has to build the physical shit and it aont be KI

62346901
>>62342700
never change, anon. I enjoy your little gay larps. i'm your fan, you're funny as fuck, I wish we had a biz party somewhere, you'd be the soul of the party unironically speaking

62346960
>>62346901
What larp?

62346974
>>62344611
>trust fund leeches.
Kek. Seething working class.
>>62344811
> How poorfag coded are you people that you think just maxing out a couple of credit cards is a lot of money
Not poor but maxing out one credit card is a lot of money.

62349408
>>62342798
bro you're onto something.
Fake accounts FLOOD the internet. Especially kinky shit like fetlife and the kink subreddits - they will have the exact same contact for totally different account and profiles.
I found 2 accounts that had each other as friends.. they had similar profiles, said they "knew each other online" and 1 used a keyword that the other one was talking to me and using..
there is a MASSIVE psyop happening.

62349422
>>62342700
OP, you clearly haven't heard this enough, you're a faggot.

62351731
>>62346960
everything that you post (on an almost daily basis) that never actually happened outside your autistic mongoloid mind, champ. I love these stories about the "middle class" lol. Keep up the good work soldier. thanks for the laughs (unironically speaking)

item.title: So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. — 31 replies — 9.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo.

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-11 19:46 UTC

OP

So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. How the fuck is it legal for them to destroy so much shareholder value? I WANT MY FUCKING MONEY BACK NOW!!

Early replies (first 3)

62348099
>>62348037
You did not lose anything, shoe shine boy. change your diapers and calm the FUCK down. Because you didn't sell yet. Right?

62348149
Better price to buy m8. You don't believe in the business anymore or somethin

62348170
If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead, I don't know what to tell you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62348177
>>62348037
nintendo is dead. the new pokemon games all have a super weird art style

62348263
>>62348170
I couldn't tell, because I stopped paying attention (and let it die i guess)

Is there anything these days like Minecraft Halloween update, the summer of dayz, or starceaft brood war Use Map Settings maps?

62348278
>>62348037

Are you the same retard that asked about buying them after an entire thread told you they were cooked? I remember giving clear examples of why this company is dogshit, specifically not owning Pokémon 100%. They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son. Sounds like you are a braindead tendie lie the rest.

62349809
>>62348099
I would have lost more if I hadn't sold, retard.

62349911
>>62348037
I FUCKING told end of last year that Nintendo was in a bubble. Yes, it's in the archives, likely with either Xenoblade pics or Rosalina pics.
>Muh P/E is useless
It isn't. As many retards will soon learn again for lot of shit they buy at overpriced prices.
Now it's at a more reasonable price, still elevated, but low enough that I'm regularly buying.

62349918
>>62349911
nintebdo movies will each make a bil

62349956
the next ubisoft

62349957
>>62348037
you could have made 100k scalping pokemon cards, wtf are you doing OP

62349964
>>62348037
Japan as a society is super conservative and stuck in its way to a retarded degree, what do you expect from the land of fax machines and archipelago syndrome?

62350244
>>62349911
What stocks options are you buying bro?

62350352
>>62348037
>all good games are made by indie developers
why would you bet on any of the big companies? they are creatively bankrupt bloated bureaucracies

62350621
Thanks for piquing my interest. Now I am off to do some research. What exactly is it that they are doing to piss you off? Other than losing sole rights to Pokémon cards?

62350665
>>62348037
I tried to tell you.

No, literally, I tried to. I was the one /v/ fag screaming at you that nintendo was on its way down the same path that killed ubisoft and ruined video games.

62350696
>>62348037
slurp the dip

62350697
>>62348037
>actually investing real money in Bing Bing wahoo

62350703
>>62350665
I am not too familiar with the video game industry. I considered to buy Ubisoft then decided not to a while ago. Had other things that ended up going for instead. Maybe I should wait out the tech bubble popping. But I do love pre-dumped stocks.

62350739
Zooming out this just looks like it is at the 2022-3 lows. The bull run happened last year. Investing in an economy in another country. Their bull runs will often have a different timing. However this can clearly go down a lot more.

62350816
>>62348037
Did you use the Switch 2 for a bunch of hours? Yes? So you saw a device with a 2-hour battery life and no games, but you still decided to go all-in on this company?

62350869
Nintendo used to put out sub par hardware that still managed to be hugely successful in the console wars, they had the handheld market cornered, 3ds was kino. How the decision to kill and eat Gameboy with a retarded tablet console got through will always be perplexing to me.

62350907
>>62350816
Ouch. Released in 2025. That caused it to dump. No wonder that bull run was so short. Nintendo bull runs usually last over a year and don't go down that fast. I think this company is recoverable but I do not know what they plan on doing to make better products. Will this be like HP and Ubisoft being shit and making bad decisions until they get a new CEO and clean house? Scratch that. Forgot what was going on at Ubisoft that made me 2nd guess buying.

62351015
Oh now I know why I didn't invest in Ubisoft is because the different ticker types were too confusing. That on top of shady incompetence going on right now.

62351629
stop buying with emotions and hopes
rape these companies like they would rape you

gaming companies at release you always short. even TTWO

62351972
>>62348037
Why are chinks like this?

62352012
>>62350907
Switch 2 is probably just a transition console before their next gimmick, like the 3DS or Wii U. Still, it could have been done much better.

62352040
>>62348263
Travis Scott had a concert in Fortnite.

62352596
>>62352012
This is always part of the problem with investing in companies normies have heard of and are emotional. I am beginning to preferer investing in reindustrialization. But videogame companies sure are damn fun to invest in from the looks of it. I was getting bored to death looking up the next best machinery parts company to invest in. Too poor to invest in all of them that aren't complete shit or too high and hope for the best. Have to really narrow it down to a few at some point. Very tedious. Especially for an average retail fag. But Nintendo and Ubisoft I can afford multiple shares.

62352642
>>62349809
Lol. Not if you just keep holding, idiot. But that's what you get for investing in bing bing wahoo slop.

62352731
>>62350352
I don't know Apple had a big ass rebound thanks to Steve Jobs. Long history old companies having a comeback after years if not decades of mismanagement.

item.title: How hard is EE uni? — 37 replies — 9.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
How hard is EE uni?

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-11 19:06 UTC

OP

How hard is EE uni?

Early replies (first 3)

62339490
>>62339486
AI, it's over. Only get licensing for a profession that is protected by the highest levels of corruption.

62339492
very, if you are not comfortable with math and physics, or don't have an iq of at least 120, you will be overwhelmed by the workload and labs and assignments

62339496
>>62339492
Where do the 115-118 IQ engineers go when they realize they're not the next Nikola Tesla?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339546
>>62339496
If they're still at university? Comp-sci. I'm not being a smartass. I was in a EE/comp-sci blended program. Had several classmates go, "I can't deal with the physics prereqs," and jump ship.

62339559
>>62339546
i don't even know why people compare comp sci with ee, comp sci is largely theoretical, most of the practical stuff you learn at work

62339563
>>62339542
ees won't be replaced like programmers, but i don't think new jobs will be created, they will just go to the current crop of engineers, most of the practical maintenance work will still be around, if you really want to hedge your bet, then do nursing or something related, that will likely be the last job that remains in demand

62339565
>>62339546
Huh I thought maybe they'd switch to mechanical engineering

62339569
>>62339486
It was very hard for me. Spent a lot of time having to study for complex math, circuit diagrams, fourier transforms, it was a fucking nightmare. Graduated at the tail end of the great recession, literally no one was hiring. Took a fucking glorified helpdesk job, worked my way up to DevOps because my degree was technically also CompEng, shifted to a different company focusing more on the FinOps side of things, and now I just do DevOps full time. Literally nothing to do with anything I spent 4 years of my life perfecting only to just throw it all away doing cloud work. Had an opportunity to start at micron a decade ago (talk about missed opportunities) but I probably wouldn't have gotten any stock options as a new grad so who knows.
Don't do this unless you want to. I enjoyed it, but what I got out of it more than anything was learning to work with Saudis and Chinks since those were my classmates, but don't even think about it being an instant golden ticket. Life's a journey so don't kick yourself if you have to work from the bottom up.

62339592
>>62339559
I think it's because they attract a lot of the same potential students: "I like computers and electronics." I can only speak for my experience, but the comp sci curriculum involved was mostly practical or foundational stuff: architecture, operating systems, networking, etc. Not the more esoteric math end of it. The mix with EE intended for targeting FPGAs, embedded, robotics, and automation/controls stuff. Basically, "know how to filter a ADC on either side."
Students would take intro to circuits and intro to programming the same year and decide they liked writing Hello World more than calculating Thevenin equivalents.

62339598
>>62339563
Oh boy are you deluded if you think you're some kind of special exception to the rule. You're already obsolete.

Muh Engineering.
Muh special skill.

Bye bye.

62339604
>>62339598
i am not saying they won't be replaced, i am saying it won't happen like programming since its a very wide field

62339621
>>62339592
i didn't know computer scientists do control, although its probably very introductory stuff, and fpgas? How do they even teach you this stuff without understanding circuits at a deeper level, or maybe they only teach how to program them and not how to design an fpga in a pcb,

62339677
Learn analog/digital hardware and some embedded programming, then try to get into industries where they require you to build highly specialized and custom embedded electronics, very lucrative but probably requires touching grass to even begin to know where the opportunities are. In electronics, you can pretty much teach yourself everything just get KiCad and read some Altium blogs to figure out what to do with traces and layer mangement, then send your gerber to some fab in China and you have a finished product. Still need real industry experience and good hardware skills to know what products are actually in demand. Stay away from copying the cool "projects" you see on Youtube, a lot of them are just performative LARPing and the concepts you learn from doing those projects might not be as applicable to what's actually needed in industry.


Never go into utilities or power engineering, the boomers stay there for 50+ years and refuse to to leave, even then they do they'll just hire some 24 year old son of some senior director to replace the boomer that just left.

62339686
>>62339677
Maybe that's still viable, but you need some moat against customers letting AI design & order the product for them. Risk & scale, for example.

62339741
>>62339686
If the AI is good enough build the product from scratch and order the proper components from the correct vendors then it's over. Doesn't matter how hard people cry about it, even if you beg for the government to ban AI to "SAVE MUH JOBS" it won't matter because the Chinese definitely won't give a shit and will go full AI adoption, and in the end the customers will end up ordering from them like they already do with majority of manufactured goods. I wouldn't worry about it until it happens. Just learn something else. If you can do it with EE, then you can do it with something else.

62339767
>>62339741
It can map very complex patterns in IT, and work autonomously for hours. That's because the data is good enough and public, IT cucks lack testosterone. Now AI corporations have access to the IP of every industry. If these API calls can be meaningfully used for training, they will find a way to force the customers to allow it, or just use it and get away with it because it's too profitable. I'd expect 12 to 48 months for end to end EE product development, depending on the political plays.

62339775
>>62339542
EE doesnt need unions. AI is only a threat to shit tier programmers. AI cant into embedded. Its hilarious to watch people try and use AI to make anything that connects to the physical world. It can make slop game, apps, and websites.

62339796
>>62339767
>the data is good enough and public, IT cucks lack testosterone.
the moat would be lucrative opportunities in fields that filter out the IT cucks where you have to service equipment outdoors where there's no ac and where the data is not good enough due to harsh operating conditions because average AI slop product would just get completely torn apart. Instrumentation for downhole drilling for example.

62339951
>>62339796
>harsh, torn apart
So we are back to fighting for a few jobs where the slaves are worked to death? Nice.

62342082
what am I gonna do.

62342136
>>62339542
AI is already used to make better chips. EEs don't give a fuck and just tell each other to git gud.

62342185
>>62339563
I'll just be making a little more than min wage as a nurse. Hours will be long too. I guess it's better than nothing.

62342217
>>62342082
https://ocw.mit.edu/
Teach yourself if you got the brains.
10 months ago I knew nothing. Now I can diagnosis pretty much any circuit AC/DC. I repair guitar amps, music equipment. Design circuits, make them.
look up the syllabus then search textbook_name.pdf. I use AI to make me questions to test myself.
There are lectures on youtube as well.

62342229
>>62342185
registered nurses make over 200k, it's a lucrative job and in extreme high demand

62342598
>>62339486
pretty easy and boring, should be harder desu; my iq is only 105 or so
however it's not optimal if you want to get chicks in college

62342635
>>62339496
industrial engineering or business and dad gets them a job

62344616
>>62339496
I work to live, not live to work. I want a Porsche and a house. Make enough to retire early.

62344643
>>62339496
Compsci, so you're still useful despite AI.

62348450
>>62339486
BS in EE isn't that bad if you're not a brainlet autist. i maintained some semblance of a social life and worked part time, GPA 3.7. Graduated 2020 EE/CE, now a senior design engineer in the med device space. Admittedly easier for me due to locale being a mecca for med design.
>>62339677
Seconded - get into regulated industry, med device if you have a soul, oil/gas or defense if you don't. AI adoption will be partial at best with human centered input and review (per FDA guidance, would assume similar for DOD and DOE) and the design cycles for something like a clinical prototype require consideration far beyond the scope of prompt engineering.
>>62342136
Exactly, its a git gud world, find a niche, git gud at it, stay at the front of what's coming.

Final advice would be to not neglect soft skills, i'm good, but being able to talk has taken me further than my technical ability. Especially true for client facing design roles or consulting work (down the line for you). I work with total autists who mask hard and have spent years hacking their own brains to make it work. Talking PhD in EE having to sell design services to finance dorks. If he can do it, you can too.

62348666
>>62342217
Do you ever feel demoralized when you realize the knowledge you teach yourself won't make you a buck?

62349072
>>62339486
Electrical Engineering? You're asking this on /biz/, though you'd probably just get trolled on /sci/. You just need to go through the foundations. Your university should have the most credible say on where you stand and your remediation placement, even above individual professors, which you should ignore if they claim you have unrealistic deficiencies that would require you to redo high school rather than quickly review a few pages, especially later on, when, assuming it's true, they created it. The things I had the most difficulty with were factorization (make sure to learn special products, calculus will have you doing them in reverse) and geometric concepts like the ambiguous/law of sines and cosines (because my professor lied and claimed it was about similar triangles, something our book only touches twice in very obscure off-topic word problem Sullivan likes to troll students with), trigonometric equations, and polar equations (because our professor told us to avoid taking notes to pay attention, but his PDF files were empty). In the end, you'll only be able to review the book method, so don't depend on crappy methods your professors might come up with. Remember, your passing grade is definitive proof you learned it, and nobody can just talk their way into taking it from you.

62351940
>>62339486
Depends on how intelligent you are. For you it’ll likely be very difficult

62352482
an actual answer? you have to learn everything - physics, math, probablility, programming, circuits. Back before my time meant it could lead you to a job doing anything. Once I was actually in it and 2008 hit, it meant I had mastered nothing. If you are ok after working ur ass off for the degree while being lumped together in the same group as tradie electricians while working and taking your lunches in the middle of nowhere then by all means. If you wanna work in a nice air conditioned room and be able to have fancy lunches whenever u want downtown, then just do CS. But most likely since you're here, u just care about the money. CS can work but seriously just get a business degree

item.title: I need financial advice from /bongbiz/, should I prioritise a Stocks ISA or Cash ISA? — 10 replies — 8.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
I need financial advice from /bongbiz/, should I prioritise a Stocks ISA or Cash ISA?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-11 21:56 UTC

OP

I need financial advice from /bongbiz/, should I prioritise a Stocks ISA or Cash ISA?

Early replies (first 3)

62350281
S&S ISA - Buy SPDR MSCI ACWI and forget.

Pension first for the tax relief though if you believe there will be a Britain worth living in, in 30-40 years and they won't fuck you up by changing the rules and tax around it. (I don't).

62350292
>>62350269
S&S Isa. Set up a standing order to automatically purchase whatever ammount you can afford monthly into a low fees ETF of your choice. Forget about it and come back in 30 years

62350316
bong here

S&S ISA is the only vehicle worth a damn

fuck everything else, and i'm probably getting out of this hellhole at some point anyway

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62350320
>>62350269
S&S ISA hands down. Returns on cash ISA's are shit. Like another anons have said in this thread, even investing in safe ETFs will bring you better returns.

Personally I do all my longs through my S&S ISA. Started with 1k back in October, up to 15k now. But I pick my own stocks and don't really touch ETFs.

62350322
Girl

62350326
I told you he is stalking you he is never getting dunkin donuts at this hour. Do fucking love me yet??????? Damn. Buy the dip though knicks in 5. Miss u

62350329
>>62350320
Even if you're peak boomer and think safe ETFs are too risky because ooo shares scawy some providers let you buy bonds and bond heavy funds anyway which can be higher return than cash ISAs and probably even safer than the banks if you have more than the deposit limit.

62352613
Cash ISAs are a joke and I agree with Rachel that they should be banned.

62353289
Stocks and shares ISA, risk turned up to the maximum option also

item.title: Would I be able to get a business loan while making child support payments? — 27 replies — 7.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Would I be able to get a business loan while making child support payments?

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-06-11 19:25 UTC

OP

Would I be able to get a business loan while making child support payments? I need a second source of income ASAP

Early replies (first 3)

62349128
women truly are the worst gains goblins.

62349399
>>62349116
GAME OVER, better luck next time

62349403
>>62349116
Kill women. Behead women. Throw women off a building

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349448
>>62349116
Keith Jones, Jr. Is that you?

62349455
>>62349116
>85/hrs and only $163 net pay after all deductions?\

How is this legal? This is modern slavery, you would be worse off than a crack head collecting bottles off the side of the road with that pay. Can’t you just claim mental illness and go on welfare and let the state pay for the childcare

62349478
>>62349116
>$2500 gross
>16% goes to taxes
>4x "support order" = $1600 in total to survive
do americans really?
also, someone explain the image pls

62349497
>>62349478
Jews and roasties

62349521
those support payments are insane, but all the money including taxes is stolen. if i received a paystub like this i would simply resign and work under the table. seriously why even play this game? the system is designed to fuck you.
quit, pull your money out of banks, hide it in the mattress. only deal in cash. work off the books. you have nothing to lose at this point.

62349620
>>62349116
Why haven't you killed your ex wife yet?

62349661
>>62349116
I refuse to believe this image is real.

62349662
>>62349116
Kek, laughting at any and all married retards. This is the future of half of married cucks on this board.

62349676
>>62349116
>Would I be able to get a business loan while making child support payments?
Your child support is irrelevant to a business loan

however to get a business loan you DO need a business. Usually they want to see 3 years of profit from your business and even then you'll need perfect business credit score and a paid-off house to put up as additional collateral.

business loans are among the hardest loans to get. They're essentially just a short-term mortgage on your house, and it better be paid off.

62349680
>>62349116
also of course if you don't own a house I wouldn't even bother. You can ask a banker. Probably should ask a banker. But every time I've asked a banker they wanted to see 3 years P&L's for the business and the deed to my house.

62349682
>>62349116
You're SUPPOSED to take care of your kids you dumb motherfucker!

62349688
>>62349455
The system is rigged.

62349728
>>62349661
There's a strict cap on how much total child support the state can take, and it's a lot less than that.

so one of three things is happening here-
1. The image is fake
2. All of the child support is taken from a single paycheck, leaving the next paycheck(s) for the month with $0 withdrawn
3. OP used to make a lot more money and chose to quit a higher paying job for a much lower paying one. This will result in the child support payments going over the legal cap, because as a divorced parent you're not allowed to purposefully make less money to avoid child support.

62349756
>>62349116
>>62349455
>>62349521
>>62349728
I don't know if OP's image is real or he just owed months of back support but I known several guys who would rather get welfare than work and give a penny to their ex.

62349764
>>62349756
Yep I've met a few like that also

usually working odd jobs for cash. Probably lost their driver's license, and will never be able to work a regular job again, but a man's gotta survive.

62349786
>>62349116
have you tried using a fucking condom

62349807
>>62349728
From my experience it's usually number 3. My former colleague used to work as a welder for $150kUSD. He has 4 or 5 kids, I don't remember anymore. His wife divorced him and his support was calculated from those 150k. A few months after, he lost his job. After some time he managed to land a job in a company I work at as some helper for around $20/h. He used to constantly complain about having nothing left from his paycheck. When I asked him why he doesn't go to the court for child support revision, he just said, "Nah, I wouldn't do that to 'em." That's it. Like yeah, I kind of respect the dedication, but man. He is 50+, living with his half-dead mother while paying a gargantuan amount of money to some whore who blows bbc in some flyover shithole.

62349829
>>62349756
I worked with a black guy that had 4 or 5 kids by 4 women, his checks were exactly like that. work 60 -70 hours a week and take home way less than $200

62349839
>>62349829
This is a good thing.

62352387
>>62349829
>have 4 or 5 kids by 4 women
that’s what I thought op was when I saw multiple child support taken. It’s either back owed support or op is black with many baby mommas

62352403
>>62349662
You do know the jewish court system orders child support if you have any kids and a roastie wants money, not even just if you were married.

62352410
>>62349478
If you bust a nut in a foid and make a babby, the foid can take you to court to force you to pay for the babby for 18 years. They dont even have to prove its your kid or anything.

62352554
>>62349116
you need to just change countries or make illegal income

62352579
>>62349399
chekt and rekt. anon is right though. sorry for your loss OP try again in 18 years
